* v1.3 Inline Widget Architecture — Phases 19-22 by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/10

* fix(widget): thread getCurrentCode into startAutoReview so AI review sees widget code by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/13
* fix: BRAT #2 multi-line stdout + #3 widget Cmd-/ comment toggle by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/14
* fix: BRAT widget cursor + multi-pane preview leaf-targeting (v1.3.0-beta.5) by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/15
* feat(search): quick problem search via palette + double-shift by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/17
* fix(graph): add Monotonic Queue to AI pattern taxonomy by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/18
* fix(auth): capture cookies via URL filter, surface timeout state by @LikeSundayLikeRain in https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/pull/19

releases.name: v1.3.0-beta.7

releases.author: LikeSundayLikeRain
releases.description:
## Bug fix release

Backports the fix for #16 (embedded login captures cookies but plugin remains logged out) to the 1.0.x line.

### What changed
- `src/auth/BrowserWindowLogin.ts`: switch cookie capture from `cookies.get({ domain: '.leetcode.com' })` to `cookies.get({ url: 'https://leetcode.com/' })` so host-only `csrftoken` cookies are no longer dropped.
- Add a 30s capture-timeout watchdog with a distinct user-facing Notice ("Login appeared to succeed but cookies could not be captured — try the manual paste fallback in Settings.") so the next silent-failure mode is visible instead of looking like a cancellation.
- New unit test `tests/browser-window-login-filter.test.ts` regression-locks the filter shape.

### Why
LeetCode sometimes returns `csrftoken` as a host-only cookie (no `Domain` attribute → stored under `leetcode.com`, not `.leetcode.com`). The previous filter omitted it, the cookie pipeline returned null on every poll, and login silently failed with no usable error to the user.

Closes #16.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.2.0-alpha.4 — Sync stability + opt-out toggle

Maintenance alpha focused on **fixing sync corruption** seen in alpha.3 plus a new **opt-out toggle** for users who prefer Obsidian's native markdown editor.

### Added

- **Settings → Code editor → Use nested code editor** toggle. When OFF, the plugin skips registering the nested CM6 child-editor at onload — Run / Submit / Reset / Retrieve / AI commands still work via the markdown fallback path. Reload Obsidian to apply changes.
- **AI solution prompt** now includes the LeetCode starter code so the generated solution matches the expected class/method signature.

### Fixed

- **Sync corruption** when the parent document and child editor diverged. Manifested as reversed/interleaved text after focus changes (e.g. `int res = 0;;0 = ser tni`). Added a divergence guard that does a full-replace sync when offsets diverge, plus trailing-newline normalization on all full-replace paths.
- **Reset code** occasionally produced `}``` ` (fence closer merged with the last brace) — auto-repair would then inject a duplicate closer. Trailing newlines now normalized on all sync paths.
- **Fence closer disappearing** when deleting trailing empty lines inside the code editor.
- **Cursor visibility on re-focus** in non-vim mode after Obsidian's `addProperty` (Cmd+;) hotkey stole focus to a property input. Pipe cursor now always renders as a fallback.

### Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) in Obsidian
2. Add beta plugin: `LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode`
3. Enable the plugin

If you've previously installed alpha.3 via BRAT, BRAT will auto-update to alpha.4.

### Bundle

- Raw: 1.72 MB (under 1.8 MB ceiling)
- Gzipped: ~460 KB

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/compare/1.2.0-alpha.3...1.2.0-alpha.4
releases.name: v1.2.0-alpha.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.2.0-alpha.3 — Code Editor Experience

The v1.2 milestone transforms the `## Code` fence into a proper code editor. This alpha includes the full nested editor, a redesigned action row, and polished settings UI.

### Nested Code Editor

A CM6 EditorView replaces the raw fence body with real editing capabilities:

- **Syntax highlighting** for all 8 LeetCode languages (Python, Java, C++, C, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Rust)
- **Smart editing** — Tab/Shift-Tab indent, auto-indent after `{`/`:`, bracket auto-close, Cmd-/ comment toggle
- **Vim mode** — Normal-mode keys stay in the code editor; Scope-based keystroke isolation
- **Fence auto-recovery** — deleted fence closers are restored automatically
- **Relative line numbers** — optional plugin setting for vim-native navigation
- **Language switching** — chevron dropdown instantly reconfigures all editor behavior
- **Bidirectional sync** — child editor stays in lock-step with the parent markdown document

### Action Row

- Unified pill-shaped buttons with icons: ✨ AI solution, ▶ Run, ☁ Submit
- New icon-only buttons: `{}` retrieve last submission, ↺ reset to starter code
- AI solution keeps purple gradient, Submit uses Obsidian accent color
- Language selector restyled: minimal text + chevron, no background

### Settings

- Grouped cards matching native Obsidian style
- **AI coach** section with top-level enable toggle + test connection icon
- Cleaner names: "Provider", "Review on Accepted", "Pattern classification", "Contest analysis"
- "Credential source" replaces "Auth method" for Bedrock
- Default Bedrock model updated to `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6`

### Other Improvements

- **Enter to Run** — press Enter (outside textarea) or Cmd+Enter to submit the Run modal
- **Light mode fixes** — PASS badge contrast, input field backgrounds
- **Release workflow** — manifest.json version injected from git tag (no manifest-beta.json needed)

### Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) in Obsidian
2. Add beta plugin: `LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode`
3. Enable the plugin

### Bundle

- Raw: ~1.72 MB (under 1.8 MB ceiling)
- Gzipped: ~460 KB

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/compare/1.1.0-alpha.2...1.2.0-alpha.3
releases.name: v1.2.0-alpha.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.1.0-alpha.1 — Contest, AI Coach, and Preview

AI-powered coaching, virtual contest mode, and non-destructive problem preview.

### Highlights

- **Problem preview** — Single-click previews problems in a read-only tab before creating a note. Sticky "Start Problem" button, tab reuse, right-click context menu.
- **AI Provider Foundation** — 5 providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Ollama, AWS Bedrock. BYO key, masked input, Test Connection probe.
- **AI Debug** — User-triggered streaming analysis of wrong-answer or TLE verdicts with actionable suggestions.
- **AI Review** — Opt-in 3-dimension review (Approach, Efficiency, Code Style) on Accepted solutions.
- **Contest mode** — Virtual past-contest practice with timer, scoring, 4-problem solve view, and AI-powered post-contest analysis.
- **AI Knowledge Graph** — 22-pattern classifier with hub notes, difficulty-progression edges, cross-cluster Related Variants.
- **AWS Bedrock** — Full provider with SSO credential process support and auto-refresh.

### Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) in Obsidian
2. Add beta plugin: `LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode`
3. Enable the plugin

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/compare/1.0.1...1.1.0-alpha.1
releases.name: 1.1.0-alpha.1

releases.author: LikeSundayLikeRain
releases.description:
## Maintenance release

Clears the Obsidian community plugin store auto-review's lint findings so the next submission passes cleanly. **No user-visible behavior changes** apart from two minor copy tweaks listed below.

### What changed

- Removed every `obsidianmd/*` ESLint disable directive that the plugin store rejects (sentence-case, prefer-active-window-timers, prefer-active-doc, prefer-create-el, no-tfile-tfolder-cast, rule-custom-message, no-plugin-id-in-command-id) and refactored the underlying code so each rule passes clean.
- All timer / DOM call sites now route through Obsidian's popout-window-safe globals (`activeDocument`, `activeWindow`).
- `require('electron')` calls funneled through a small loader helper; runtime behavior unchanged.

### User-visible copy tweaks

- Settings heading: **"Knowledge Graph" → "Knowledge graph"** (sentence case).
- Auto-backlink toggle: **"…on Accepted" → "…on accepted"** (sentence case).

### Command id rename (potential breaking change)

- Command id `open-leetcode-browser` → `open-problem-browser`. The user-facing palette entry ("LeetCode: Open problem browser") is unchanged, but **users with a custom hotkey bound to the old id will need to re-bind it** under Settings → Hotkeys.

Verification: `npm run lint` (0 errors / 0 warnings), `npm run build`, `npm test` (651 passed, 3 skipped).

releases.author: LikeSundayLikeRain
releases.description:
## v1.0.0 — Initial release

A first-class Obsidian plugin for solving LeetCode problems without leaving the vault. Browse the LeetCode problem list, open a problem as a vault note, run code against custom test cases, submit solutions and see verdicts inline, switch fence languages from a chevron dropdown, view past submissions, and have technique tags + backlinks auto-generated on Accepted submissions.

### Highlights

- **Browse + open** — right-sidebar problem browser with search/filters, embedded login, throttled HTTP, one-click "open as note" with full frontmatter + Markdown problem statement.
- **Solve** — Run/Submit inline action row in both Reading and Edit mode, multi-tab Run modal with editable test cases, polling-based verdict modal, chevron dropdown for atomic language switch.
- **Past Submissions** — list view with verdict pills + runtime/memory, syntax-highlighted detail modal, "Copy to ## Code" + "Copy failing testcase" affordances.
- **Knowledge graph** — auto-merge LC topic tags as `lc/{slug}`, append `[[Technique]]` wikilinks under `## Techniques`, configurable backlink folder + opt-out toggle.
- **Section locking** — plugin-owned regions (`## Problem`, fence opener/closer, headings) are read-only in Edit mode to prevent accidental edits getting clobbered. Cursor snaps out of locked regions; selections still allow copying.
- **Offline cache** — previously fetched problems readable without internet.

### Installation

Until accepted into the Obsidian community plugin store, install manually:

1. Download `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` from this release.
2. Copy them into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/leetcode/`.
3. Open Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → enable **LeetCode**.

After enabling, go to Settings → LeetCode → **Log in** to capture your LeetCode session cookie.

### Tech stack

- TypeScript ^5.8.3 + esbuild bundler
- Obsidian plugin API + CodeMirror 6
- `@leetnotion/leetcode-api` for GraphQL; hand-rolled REST for run/submit/check
- 652 tests passing, ~163 KB production bundle

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/LikeSundayLikeRain/obsidian-leetcode/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for the full feature list, locked decisions, and known limitations.
